scope: Gate analyze_rust_file's Panic emission (T4 trigger at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:574-590) behind a default-suppressed CLI flag --include-smells on VulnArgs. Add the flag definition (default false) at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:73-101 (mirror include_informational at L92 precedent). Add a filter step in VulnArgs::run filter pipeline (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:180-196) parallel to the existing include_informational filter at L194-196. Filter predicate: `f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic")` — tight title-prefix to avoid suppressing a hypothetical future canonical-pipeline Panic emission with a different title. NO change to analyze_rust_file body or signature. NO change to is_rust_test_file. NO new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants. NO public-API shape change. NO JSON / SARIF schema change. The other 6 analyze_rust_file triggers (UnsafeCode T1, MemorySafety T2/T3/T6, SqlInjection T5, CommandInjection T7) continue emitting unconditionally per investigation.json §verified_facts.analyze_rust_file_emit_categories. Recommended Option A from plan.md §3; Options B (smell-level enum) / C (delete Panic emission) / D (taint-cross-reference) explicitly rejected per §3 rationale. Closes ZERO existing RED tests; improves production UX on Rust codebases per rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 R2 sub-elephant.
closes_carry_forward: rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 R2 sub-elephant (per-.unwrap() Panic flood on production Rust codebases) — hardening, not a RED test closure.
enables_followons: panic-taint-cross-ref-v1 (Option D from plan §3 — long-term: cross-reference unwrap-Panic emission with canonical taint pipeline; requires new TaintSinkType and threading taint state into analyze_rust_file)
smells-level-tier-v1 (Option B fallback — if/when ≥3 smell triggers exist, migrate the bool flag to a tier enum)
validator_verdict: PENDING_PREMORTEM (planning self-assessed PASS_AUTONOMOUS_READY; awaiting /premortem on this dispatch contract)

validator_mandates.no_breaking_api_change_to_json_schema: VulnFinding struct shape (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/types.rs) unchanged. Per-finding JSON keys unchanged: vuln_type, severity, cwe_id, title, description, file, line, column, taint_flow, remediation, confidence. SARIF rules array enumerated from emitted findings — entries reflect findings present (presence/absence is content, not schema). NO new fields. NO removed fields. M2 stop-threshold runs `tldr vuln <fixture> --format json` and verifies key set unchanged via diff against an M1-captured baseline.
validator_mandates.default_suppressed_panic: VulnArgs::include_smells defaults to bool false. When omitted from CLI invocation, the filter step in VulnArgs::run drops findings where `f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic")`. M2 stop-threshold gates on test_vuln_run_default_suppresses_panic_findings GREEN — asserts ZERO Panic findings on a fixture containing both .unwrap() and unsafe { }, AND ≥1 UnsafeCode finding (regression guard for accidental over-gating).
validator_mandates.panic_recoverable_via_flag: Passing `--include-smells` (or programmatically VulnArgs::include_smells = true) bypasses the filter step. Legacy Panic emission is restored. M2 stop-threshold gates on test_vuln_run_with_include_smells_emits_panic_findings GREEN — asserts ≥1 Panic finding emerges with the flag set on the same fixture as test_vuln_run_default_suppresses_panic_findings.
validator_mandates.preserve_unsafecode_memorysafety_emissio: The 6 non-Panic triggers (T1 UnsafeCode, T2/T3/T6 MemorySafety, T5 SqlInjection, T7 CommandInjection per investigation.json §analyze_rust_file_emit_categories) emit unconditionally regardless of --include-smells. Predicate is title-prefix + vuln_type-bound to Panic; cannot accidentally over-match. M2 stop-threshold runs `tldr vuln <fixture-with-unsafe>` (no flag) and verifies UnsafeCode finding emerges. M3 binary smoke independently re-verifies on a representative Rust fixture.
validator_mandates.no_test_analyze_rust_signature_churn: analyze_rust_file body and signature UNCHANGED. The 6 prefix-matching test_analyze_rust_* tests (vuln.rs:999, 1010, 1029, 1044, 1057, 1070) call analyze_rust_file directly with no flag plumbing. ZERO existing test signature changes. The 1 Panic-asserting existing test (test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code at vuln.rs:1057) continues asserting findings.iter().any(|f| f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic) — analyze_rust_file still emits Panic; the filter only applies in VulnArgs::run. M2 stop-threshold runs `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` and verifies all 9 GREEN.
validator_mandates.no_enum_variant_extension: VulnType (CLI + core), TaintSinkType, TaintSourceType, Severity — no new variants. is_smell_finding helper (if introduced) is a free function with body `f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic")`. NO new types.
validator_mandates.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: All milestones internal. Local tag `rust-panic-suppression-v1` only at M4. NO `cargo publish`, NO `git push`, NO version bump in Cargo.toml. USER STANDING RULE.
validator_mandates.no_cfg_test_masking: Per user's no-gaming/skipping rule (CLAUDE.md): the suppression is gated via runtime CLI flag, NOT via #[cfg(test)] / #[allow(...)] / conditional compilation. Reviewable code path; user-controllable behavior. M2 worker MUST NOT add #[cfg(test)] or #[cfg(feature = ...)] to the filter step or the predicate.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | awk '/continuum\/autonomous\// { print }' | grep -v 'rust-panic-suppression-v1-plan/' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.verification_note: Mirrors rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 staging convention. Manual `git show HEAD --stat` audit is canonical.
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts (planning-time `git status` shows unstaged modifications across vuln-migration-v1-plan/, regex-removal-v1-plan/, sanitizer-removal-v1-plan/, etc.). Cargo.lock NEVER staged.

milestones.scope: Run baseline test captures at HEAD: (1) `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` — expect 9/9 GREEN. (2) `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red` — expect 167/168 GREEN (the 1 RED is the Bucket B / Ruby carry-forward, untouched here). This is the M1 baseline against which M3 measures the count drop. (4) Confirm investigation.json §verified_facts still hold against the source at HEAD (re-grep VulnArgs field count, analyze_rust_file trigger count, is_rust_test_file body, test inventory line numbers). (5) Verify NO modifications were made to analyze_rust_file post-rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 M2 (the dispatch flip touched analyze_file, not analyze_rust_file). (6) Capture verbatim CLI help output: `tldr vuln --help` — pre-M2 baseline so M3 can diff the help text for the new flag.

167/168 vuln_migration_v1_red tests confirmed GREEN at HEAD; the 1 RED is documented (NOT touched by this milestone).

tldr vuln binary smoke on crates/tldr-cli/src/ AND crates/tldr-core/src/ records baseline: total finding count, per-vuln_type histogram, count of Panic findings (the suppression target), count of UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Sql/Cmd findings (regression guards).
investigation.json verified_facts re-confirmed at HEAD (line numbers may shift if HEAD has drifted; M1 worker MUST update if so and surface in M1-report.json).

milestones.title: ATOMIC: Add --include-smells flag + Panic-emission filter step + 2 new tests
milestones.scope: In crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs: (1) add field `pub include_smells: bool` to VulnArgs at L73-101 with `#[arg(long)] pub include_smells: bool` — mirror include_informational at L92. Place the new field after `include_informational` for visual grouping. Doc comment: `/// Include code-smell findings (e.g., per-.unwrap() Panic emissions on Rust files). Default: false (smells suppressed).`. (2) Add a filter step in VulnArgs::run after the include_informational filter at L194-196: `if !self.include_smells { filtered_findings.retain(|f| !is_smell_finding(f)); }`. (3) Add a private free function `fn is_smell_finding(f: &VulnFinding) -> bool { f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic") }` — placed near analyze_rust_file or near the filter pipeline (worker's discretion; keep it close to ONE of the two for grep-locality). (4) Add 2 new #[test] fns in the commands::remaining::vuln::tests module: (a) test_vuln_run_default_suppresses_panic_findings — uses tempfile::TempDir to create a .rs file with both `.unwrap()` and `unsafe { ... }` (without SAFETY comment), invokes VulnArgs::run with default flag values, parses the JSON output, asserts ZERO Panic findings AND ≥1 UnsafeCode finding. (b) test_vuln_run_with_include_smells_emits_panic_findings — same fixture, with VulnArgs::include_smells = true; asserts ≥1 Panic finding emerges. Both tests construct VulnArgs directly (mirror test_collect_files_includes_rust idiom at L986). ATOMIC: flag def + filter step + helper + 2 new tests + any required imports ship in ONE commit.
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: +45 to +75 LOC (~5 LOC field def + doc, ~3 LOC filter step, ~3 LOC helper fn, ~25 LOC test_vuln_run_default_suppresses_panic_findings, ~25 LOC test_vuln_run_with_include_smells_emits_panic_findings, ~5 LOC imports if needed)

test_vuln_run_with_include_smells_emits_panic_findings GREEN — verifies flag round-trip restores legacy emission.

All 9 pre-existing #[test] fns in commands::remaining::vuln::tests STAY GREEN — verified by `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests` (expect 11/11 GREEN total: 9 pre-existing + 2 new).

If this test regresses, M2 implementation has wrongly placed the gate inside analyze_rust_file — STOP and re-examine layer choice.

milestones.title: Verification, binary smoke, JSON delta capture
milestones.scope: Build release binary with --features semantic at the M2 SHA. Run binary smoke on the SAME corpora as M1: (1) `tldr vuln crates/tldr-cli/src/ --lang rust --format json` (default flags). Record total finding count + per-vuln_type histogram. Compare to M1 baseline counts. (2) Same invocation with `--include-smells`. Record counts; verify Panic count matches M1 baseline (round-trip). (3) Repeat (1)+(2) on crates/tldr-core/src/. (4) Construct a synthetic Rust fixture with EXACTLY: 1 unsafe { without SAFETY comment, 1 .unwrap(), 1 mem::transmute( call, 1 std::ptr::read( call, 1 from_utf8_unchecked(. Run `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust --format json` (no flag); assert exactly 0 Panic, ≥1 UnsafeCode, ≥1 MemorySafety (transmute), ≥1 MemorySafety (Raw Pointer), ≥1 MemorySafety (Unchecked Byte/String). (5) Same fixture with --include-smells; assert ≥1 Panic in addition to the 4 above. (6) JSON schema diff: `diff <(tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust --format json | jq -S 'first.findings | first | keys') <(tldr vuln <fixture> --lang rust --format json --include-smells | jq -S 'first.findings | first | keys')` — must be empty (key set unchanged across flag values). (7) CLI help diff: capture `tldr vuln --help` post-M2; diff against M1-cli-help-pre.txt; verify the only addition is the new `--include-smells` flag entry. Document the JSON count delta as a downstream-consumer-facing behavior change in M3-binary-smoke.json.
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: 0 source code; ~250 LOC across 4 report artifacts
stop_thresholds: Binary smoke crates/tldr-cli/src/ default invocation: total finding count is LESS THAN M1 baseline; Panic count is 0; UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Sql/Cmd counts UNCHANGED from M1 baseline.
Binary smoke crates/tldr-cli/src/ --include-smells invocation: total finding count EQUALS M1 baseline; Panic count EQUALS M1 baseline (round-trip).
Binary smoke crates/tldr-core/src/ default + --include-smells: same pattern as crates/tldr-cli/src/.
Synthetic-fixture default invocation: 0 Panic, ≥1 UnsafeCode, ≥1 MemorySafety×3 (transmute, Raw Pointer, Unchecked Byte/String).
Synthetic-fixture --include-smells invocation: ≥1 Panic plus all the above.
JSON schema diff (key set across flag values) is EMPTY — verified via `jq -S '.findings[0] | keys'` comparison.
CLI help diff: only ADDITION (no removal, no modification) is the new --include-smells flag entry.
M3-binary-smoke.json captures: M1 baseline counts (copied), M3 default counts, M3 --include-smells counts, delta tables (total + per-vuln_type), and the synthetic-fixture verbatim JSON outputs.
M3-report.json records SHA at which verification was run, all metric counts, baseline-drift check result from M1, and links to M3-binary-smoke.json + CLI help diff.

EMPIRICAL-MAGNITUDE CHECK (per plan §5 R10): if the workspace-corpus default-vs-include-smells delta is <10 findings total, M3 documents this as 'magnitude smaller than R2 sub-elephant feared' but DOES NOT block the milestone — hardening is correct in principle.

Then apply local tag `rust-panic-suppression-v1` at the CHANGELOG commit SHA. NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.
milestones.loc_delta_estimate: ~30 LOC CHANGELOG; ~50 LOC across 2 report JSONs
stop_thresholds: CHANGELOG entry merged in (NOT published — internal milestone).
Local tag `rust-panic-suppression-v1` applied at the CHANGELOG commit SHA.
`git tag --list 'rust-panic-suppression-v1'` returns the tag.
cargo build --workspace PASS (sanity check).
No push, no publish, no version bump.

rollback_plan.if_M2_fails: git revert <M2_sha>. The atomic commit means revert is single-step. Flag def + filter step + helper + 2 new tests revert atomically. The 9 pre-existing tests return to GREEN; the 2 new tests cease to exist. No downstream impact on test_e2e_* or vuln_migration_v1_red.
rollback_plan.if_M2_breaks_test_analyze_rust_detects_u: git revert <M2_sha>. This regression signals the gate was wrongly placed inside analyze_rust_file. M2 must re-implement scoping the filter to VulnArgs::run only — analyze_rust_file body must remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
rollback_plan.if_M2_breaks_unsafecode_or_memorysafety_: git revert <M2_sha>. Predicate over-suppressed (per plan §5 R7). Re-implement with tighter title-prefix or add explicit type-equality assertion to the predicate.
rollback_plan.if_M3_surfaces_jsonschema_drift: git revert <M2_sha>. Schema must be invariant across flag values. Re-examine the filter step for unintended side effects (e.g., severity-histogram drift in build_summary).
rollback_plan.if_M3_empirical_magnitude_below_threshol: DOCUMENT in M3-binary-smoke.json with non-additive-resolution rationale; do NOT revert. The hardening is correct in principle even if the magnitude is smaller than feared.

success_summary_post_m4.vuln_migration_v1_red: 167/168 passing (UNCHANGED from HEAD; this milestone closes ZERO RED tests).
success_summary_post_m4.commands_remaining_vuln_tests: 11/11 passing (was 9/9 at HEAD; +2 new tests for the flag round-trip).
success_summary_post_m4.json_schema_invariance: VulnFinding key set unchanged across --include-smells flag values; verified via M3 jq diff.
success_summary_post_m4.production_ux_improvement: tldr vuln on Rust codebases no longer emits per-.unwrap() Panic findings by default. Suppression magnitude quantified in M3-binary-smoke.json on tldr-code's own crates as the canonical sample corpus.
success_summary_post_m4.carry_forward_aggregate_post_m4: UNCHANGED from HEAD's 4 retained RED (Bucket B 3 nested-constructor + Bucket A Ruby 1). This milestone is hardening, not RED closure.
success_summary_post_m4.follow_on_milestones_remaining_unchanged: var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 (closes Bucket B 3); ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 (closes Bucket A Ruby 1).

post_publish_unblock: After M4 lands, the v0.2.x release group has improved production UX on Rust codebases. Combined with the existing 167/168 GREEN baseline, the published binary's `tldr vuln` will produce a less-noisy default JSON output for downstream consumers parsing Rust-codebase scans.
